text.title: Request-Execute-Respond Loop
text.summary: The tool use loop pattern: send tools to LLM, detect tool calls in response, execute, feed results back, and cap iterations to prevent runaway loops.

rule.statement: Cap every tool use loop at a fixed iteration limit (10 is a safe default) and return a partial result with a [max_iterations_reached] marker when the limit is hit — never run unbounded.
rule.rationale: If tool results never satisfy the model's reasoning, it keeps calling tools until the context overflows or costs spike. A hard cap is mandatory for production stability.

example.note: Wrap json.loads in try/except — the model occasionally produces malformed JSON arguments, especially with complex schemas.

rule.statement: Never assume OpenAI and Anthropic tool schemas are interchangeable; maintain a single tool registry and translate to provider-specific format at call time.
rule.rationale: OpenAI uses "type": "function" wrapper + "parameters"; Anthropic uses "input_schema" not "parameters"; Gemini uses protos.FunctionDeclaration. A single registry must translate between formats.

example.code: # Hardcoding Anthropic schema into OpenAI call tools = [{"name": "get_weather", "input_schema": {...}}] # wrong for OpenAI client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o", tools=tools)

rule.statement: Always include the tool_call_id (OpenAI) or tool_use_id (Anthropic) in the tool result message; omitting it causes a validation error.
rule.rationale: Both providers require correlation IDs in tool results to match calls to responses. Missing IDs fail silently or raise SDK errors depending on the provider version.
